factors.name: Semiconductor earnings miss / AI demand concerns
factors.summary: Samsung earnings failed to meet 'high AI bar' expectations, triggering broad chip sector selloff (SMH -5%). VanEck Semiconductor ETF weakness rippling across Nasdaq; Micron shares down on AI tape skepticism per MarketWatch.

factors.name: US-Iran escalation & geopolitical premium
factors.summary: US launched 'powerful strikes' on Iran; Treasury revoked Iran oil sales waiver just 19 days after granting it. Strait of Hormuz traffic halted; ceasefire unraveling. Elevated geopolitical tail risk typically pressures growth-heavy tech multiples.

factors.name: Nasdaq-100 relative weakness vs S&P/Dow
factors.summary: Nasdaq down -1.16% (302.47 pts) on the day; S&P -0.45%; Dow tracking better. Nasdaq has never escaped negative territory during session, suggesting sustained institutional selling in mega-cap tech.

factors.name: AI capex bond selloff & funding stress
factors.summary: AI-related debt sold off sharply as Amazon announced $25B new bond issuance. Suggests capex cycle may be hitting diminishing returns or investor caution on AI ROI is rising; negative for chipmakers and cloud providers.

factors.name: Real yields / duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Fed's Williams sounding more dovish on inflation as oil prices retreat from Iran-war peak. No fresh Treasury data in news window, but geopolitical premium keeping rates bid; long-duration tech multiples under pressure despite dovish Fed rhetoric.

factors.name: Dollar strength on risk-off
factors.summary: USD Index holding near 101.00 area; AUD/USD dropped 0.39% on Hormuz tension. Stronger dollar headwind for US tech exporters (Nasdaq-100 has ~40% international revenue exposure).

factors.name: Fed policy expectations shifting slightly dovish
factors.summary: Fed's Williams comments and traders now hedging for less hawkish Fed (per Bloomberg); dot plot still shows majority expecting rate hikes by year-end, but momentum toward dovish surprise reduces odds of aggressive tightening.

factors.name: Trader positioning: Kalshi bets on cooler H2 2026
factors.summary: Speculators on Kalshi predict Nasdaq-100 will end 2026 above 30,000 but see more subdued rally in second half vs first half. Suggests peak sentiment may already have arrived.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq-100 is in a post-earnings selloff driven by Samsung's AI disappointment and broad semiconductor sector weakness; this is amplified by renewed US-Iran hostilities and risk-off positioning as Strait of Hormuz traffic halts. While Fed speakers have softened tone on inflation, the combination of chip demand concerns, elevated geopolitical tail risk, and AI capex funding stress is outweighing dovish Fed rhetoric. The index has spent the entire session in negative territory, suggesting institutional conviction toward lower prices. Kalshi traders see H2 2026 as cooler, hinting that the mega-cap tech rally may be entering a consolidation or correction phase.
catalysts_ahead: RBNZ OCR decision (Wednesday 8 July) – expected 25bps hike to 2.50%; any hold or dovish guidance would ease rate-hike cycle expectations globally
Major tech earnings this week (Tesla, Nvidia, Microsoft, Amazon, Apple dates TBD) – critical to confirm capex cycle durability and AI revenue traction
Next CPI/PCE print – inflation surprise either way would re-price Fed hike odds and real yields
Strait of Hormuz status updates – de-escalation would ease oil premium and geopolitical VIX spike; further attacks would keep risk-off bid in place
Treasury yield moves – 10Y could spike on geopolitical premium; any sharp rise would compress growth multiple valuations
screening.risk_warning: This bias is invalidated if: (1) Samsung/chip weakness proves to be a single-quarter reset rather than sustained slowdown (watch Nvidia earnings for rebuttal); (2) US-Iran ceasefire is re-established quickly and Hormuz traffic resumes (oil premium collapses, geopolitical risk-off unwinds, dollar weakens, growth multiples re-expand); (3) Fed signals imminent rate cuts (real yields plunge, long-duration tech rallies 2-3%); (4) Major mega-cap tech earnings beat on AI capex ROI justification; (5) Next CPI comes in hot, causing market to reprice real yields higher and reduce fed-cut odds (initially BEARISH, but could reduce the immediate geopolitical risk premium). Watch 10Y Treasury yield and VIX closely—if VIX breaks above 25 and stays, 4h chart likely to show capitulation bottom.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (down -1.16% on session, index never escaped negative). Consider short entries only on intraday bounces to 29,400–29,500 resistance with tight stops above 29,600; safer entry is a pullback to 29,000–29,100 support zone if technical reversal signals appear. Avoid chasing lower into intraday lows as volatility and gap risk remain elevated into RBNZ and chip earnings.

factors.name: Semiconductor sector selloff
factors.summary: Samsung earnings disappointed despite AI hype, triggering broad chip selloff with VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH) down 5% per CNBC. Nasdaq down -1.16% led by tech weakness, indicating AI trade taking profits.

factors.name: AI capex concerns
factors.summary: Micron stock fell on investor skittishness about 'AI tape' per MarketWatch analyst feedback. AI-related debt sold off sharply as Amazon announced $25B bond offering, suggesting funding strain for buildout.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (Iran/Hormuz)
factors.summary: US launched 'powerful strikes' on Iran after Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks, with oil jumping and VIX likely elevated. Risk-off rotation out of long-duration tech into safe havens; Nasdaq particularly vulnerable to geopolitical spikes.

factors.name: Rate expectations
factors.summary: Fed's Williams sounded more upbeat on inflation but cautious overall. One-year inflation expectations hit highest since 2023 per NY Fed survey, keeping Fed hike pricing alive and pressuring growth multiples.

factors.name: China AI export restrictions
factors.summary: Beijing reportedly looking at curbing overseas access to China's top AI models per Stock Market News, adding regulatory uncertainty to AI trade and potential revenue headwinds for US tech operating in China.

factors.name: Mega-cap sentiment
factors.summary: Adobe called 'polarizing' amid AI questions per MarketWatch. Tesla sees 20% upside speculation on SpaceX merger but remains speculative. Micron and chip names under pressure signals broader tech rotation.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD surged amid Middle East tensions with AUD down 0.39%, gold retreating to $4,100 from Iran strikes. Stronger dollar pressures NDX multinational revenues and encourages rotation from growth to defensives.

factors.name: Oil price surge
factors.summary: WTI crude jumped on US strikes and revocation of Iran oil waiver, with Exxon reporting $3.7B profit surge. Higher energy costs feed stagflation fears, compressing tech multiples while boosting input costs.

factors.name: Options positioning
factors.summary: Traders on Kalshi think Nasdaq-100 will end 2026 above 30,000, suggesting cooler second half expected. Options traders betting broader market overestimates Fed hikes per Bloomberg, but near-term damage already done.

factors.name: ETF flows
factors.summary: Institutional investors exiting South Korean equities (KOSPI in bear market), tech-led strain per BNY. While Korea-specific, signals broader EM/tech de-risking that pressures Nasdaq components with Asia exposure.

confirmation.narrative: Nasdaq faces a confluence of headwinds: Samsung's disappointing AI-era earnings triggered a 5% semiconductor ETF selloff, exposing fragility in the AI trade that propelled YTD gains. Concurrently, escalating US-Iran conflict (fresh strikes, Hormuz vessel attacks, oil waiver revoked) sparked risk-off flows with oil surging and the dollar strengthening—both toxic for long-duration tech multiples. Rising inflation expectations (NY Fed survey highest since 2023) keep Fed hike pricing alive, while China's potential AI export curbs add regulatory uncertainty. The -1.16% Nasdaq close represents phase-2 selling; move is underway but not fully extended.
catalysts_ahead: RBNZ rate decision (likely hike to 2.50%) July 8—risk-off reinforcement if hawkish
US CPI print (date TBD but imminent)—critical for Fed trajectory and real yields
Iran conflict escalation trajectory—further Hormuz disruption could spike oil/VIX
Upcoming mega-cap earnings (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL dates unclear but Q2 season approaching)
Fed speakers this week—any hawkish tilt compounds pressure
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidates if: (1) Iran ceasefire announced and oil collapses below $75, removing stagflation premium; (2) Fed speakers turn dovish citing growth slowdown, collapsing real yields; (3) NVDA or major hyperscaler issues bullish AI demand update reversing chip selloff; (4) US CPI comes in meaningfully softer than expected, triggering rate-cut repricing. Reclaim of 29,400 with conviction would suggest dip-buying overwhelms macro concerns.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move already underway (-302 points today); wait for relief bounce to 29,300-29,400 resistance zone before considering shorts, or break below 28,850 support for trend continuation confirmation.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran strikes)
factors.summary: US launched 80+ strikes on Iran, revoked oil sales waiver, and escalated Strait of Hormuz tensions. Oil spiked to $72.20 WTI and Brent to $76.38, threatening stagflation narrative that pressures long-duration tech multiples.

factors.name: Real yields and duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Oil shock and geopolitical premium risk pushing inflation expectations higher, which compresses real yields and hits growth/tech stocks disproportionately hard. Tech carries heavy long-duration exposure.

factors.name: USD strength on safe-haven bid
factors.summary: DXY holding above 101.00 (per FXStreet, 101.10 in Asian hours). Stronger dollar headwind for Nasdaq 100 components with international revenue (MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, etc.).

factors.name: AI capex skepticism and valuation concerns
factors.summary: Multiple reports cite 'skittish AI tape' (Micron selloff, MarketWatch), Adobe polarization, Samsung earnings triggering AI hardware rotation. Risk-off mood favors value over growth mega-caps.

factors.name: Bond selloff and yield rise
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports bonds retreating across most of Asia; AI-related debt sold off sharply as Amazon eyes $25B borrowing. Rising yields compress tech multiple expansion.

factors.name: 4h change already negative
factors.summary: NAS100 down 0.43% in 4h but only +0.38% in 24h suggests late-stage volatility; bearish technical backdrop with geopolitical overhang.

factors.name: Prior analyst bias alignment
factors.summary: Your 2026-07-08 00:07 read: SELL 7/10 (Haiku), BEARISH 7/10 (Sonnet). This read represents a sustained bias, not a reversal.

factors.name: FOMC minutes imminent
factors.summary: Fed minutes in focus but not yet released; event risk into FOMC could cap further downside or trigger repricing. Introduces chop risk.

factors.name: Positive: RBNZ hike and growth signals
factors.summary: RBNZ raised OCR to 2.50%, signaling confidence inflation may have peaked. AUD, NZD rallied. Suggests some central banks moving away from panic; modest bullish cross-asset tone.

factors.name: China crude purchases and demand signals
factors.summary: China purchased 26M barrels in days, suggesting demand resilience and oil-price floor expectations. Modest cyclical support, but offset by geopolitical premium.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a classic risk-off scenario: US-Iran escalation spiked oil to $72+ WTI, triggering stagflation concerns that compress long-duration tech multiples. The dollar bid (+0.10% on DXY) and bond selloff amplify the headwind. AI capex optimism has faded into skepticism (Micron, Adobe, Samsung rotation), and your prior SELL 7/10 bias remains intact. The 4h decline of 0.43% signals momentum is already turning south, but 24h gain of +0.38% suggests the move is early-stage (PHASE_2) rather than fully extended. FOMC minutes and Fed commentary are imminent catalysts that could introduce volatility.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Minutes release (expected ~18:00 ET today, 2026-07-08) — determines inflation/rate outlook; if hawkish, NAS100 sells off further
Fed speakers (Kashkari, Barkin, etc.) — any hawkish tilt on oil shock response could accelerate selloff
Oil price action — continued Hormuz supply risk could push WTI toward $75+, keeping stagflation premium alive
Tech earnings guidance on capex/cloud demand (no specific dates in window, but Samsung rotation suggests AI demand deceleration narrative is in play)
Ceasefire outcome or de-escalation news from Iran-US talks — relief rally catalyst if tensions ease
screening.risk_warning: A credible de-escalation or ceasefire announcement could reverse the bearish stance rapidly (oil falls 5-10%, real yields compress, tech re-rates higher). Alternatively, if FOMC minutes surprise dovish or oil stabilizes, the initial weakness could be a SELL trap. Also watch for mega-cap earnings beats (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) — a single 'blowout guidance' could override macro headwinds and trigger short-covering rally.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early PHASE_2 (4h down 0.43%, catalysts ahead). Avoid chasing shorts here; wait for retest of 29300 resistance or FOMC minutes clarity before adding. If NAS100 breaks 29050 support cleanly on high volume post-FOMC, consider scaling into shorts. Conversely, hold shorts only if oil remains above $71 and dollar sustains DXY >101.00.

factors.name: US-Iran Military Escalation
factors.summary: US conducted 80+ strikes on Iranian targets; Iran retaliated with 85 strikes on US bases in Bahrain/Kuwait. Ceasefire deal collapsing, Strait of Hormuz traffic halted/severely disrupted. Major geopolitical risk-off catalyst.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike
factors.summary: Brent crude jumped 3% to $76/bbl, WTI above $72 on two-week highs. Oil shocks typically compress tech/growth multiples via stagflation fears and higher real yields.

factors.name: Real Yields & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Oil surge reignites inflation concerns; RBA's Hunter (Australia) warned supply shocks should not be overlooked, keeping hawkish bias alive. Energy price shock often triggers Fed re-pricing and long-duration NDX selloff.

factors.name: Asian Equity Weakness
factors.summary: Japan Nikkei down 1.85%, South Korea KOSPI down >5%, Australia ASX 200 down 0.21%. Broad regional selloff suggests risk-off tone spreading, with AI rotation already ongoing (cheaper China tech favored over Korea chipmakers).

factors.name: Risk-Off Mood & Crypto Weakness
factors.summary: Bitcoin slipped below $63k on risk-off sentiment; VIX/geopolitical premium lifting. NDX historically correlates risk-on/off; deteriorating crypto sentiment is early warning.

factors.name: Prior Bias History (SELL signals recent)
factors.summary: 2026-07-08 00:07 read was SELL 7/10 (Sonnet BEARISH 7/10), then SELL 6/10 at 04:13 with no confirmation. Sustained bearish reads suggest building momentum, though 6/10 indicates uncertainty.

factors.name: FOMC Minutes Upcoming (18:00 GMT Today)
factors.summary: June FOMC minutes due later today—major potential repricing catalyst for rate expectations. Market awaiting clarity on Fed's stance on energy shock and inflation risks; high uncertainty pins confidence.

factors.name: 4h/24h Price Action
factors.summary: NAS100 -0.43% in 4h, +0.38% in 24h. Small moves despite major catalyst; suggests initial move underway but not yet extended. Lack of directional conviction argues for caution.

screening.narrative: US-Iran military escalation (80+ US strikes, Iranian retaliation on bases) has reignited geopolitical risk-off and triggered an oil spike (Brent +3% to $76/bbl). Asian equities are broadly weaker (Nikkei -1.85%, KOSPI -5%), signaling contagion to US indices. Oil shocks typically compress long-duration growth multiples; inflation re-pricing fears are rising as RBA/central banks signal they won't overlook supply-side pressures. Prior SELL bias remains in place, but NDX has only fallen modestly (-0.43% in 4h), suggesting initial liquidation may not yet be fully played out or conviction is mixed ahead of FOMC minutes at 18:00 GMT today.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC June Meeting Minutes (18:00 GMT today) — major re-pricing event for rate expectations
Continued US-Iran escalation risk — any further strikes or ceasefire breakdown would amplify oil/geopolitical premium
Oil inventory data and supply flow updates from Hormuz disruption
Major tech earnings (if any scheduled in next 48-72h) — likely to be overshadowed by macro noise
screening.risk_warning: If FOMC minutes signal a dovish pivot or rate-cut readiness (lower real yields), NDX could reverse sharply higher, invalidating SELL bias. Alternatively, if geopolitical tensions de-escalate or ceasefire stabilizes within hours, oil could retreat and risk sentiment could recover. Move is still in early phase (PHASE_2) and relatively small in magnitude; early-entry shorts risk being stopped out on retest or mean reversion.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway but not extended; wait for pullback to 29300-29400 resistance zone to confirm rejection before adding shorts. FOMC minutes at 18:00 GMT is a major inflection point—avoid aggressive entries ahead of that release; consider sitting on hands or taking partial profits at key resistance levels.

factors.name: Iran ceasefire collapse & geopolitical escalation
factors.summary: Trump declared US-Iran MoU 'over' as of ~08:27 UTC. Oil spiked 3.2% to $74.30 WTI; Brent jumped above $76/bbl. This is the dominant driver of today's risk-off sentiment, with equities rotating into defensive positioning. Strait of Hormuz supply risk now renewed.

factors.name: Oil/inflation expectations re-pricing
factors.summary: Oil jump from $72 to $76+ revives inflation concerns. ECB, Fed may face renewed pressure on disinflation narrative. European bonds sold off sharply (French 10Y yields hit highest since 2009 per Bloomberg 08:02). This threatens long-duration tech multiple compression.

factors.name: Real yields / duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Oil-driven inflation fears push nominal and real yields higher. NAS100 (mega-cap, high-duration growth) is extremely sensitive to real rate spikes. Higher oil = higher Fed terminal rate expectations in near term, pressuring valuations.

factors.name: VIX / risk-aversion flows
factors.summary: Emerging markets extended losses (Bloomberg 09:00). Futures weakness across Dow (-0.40%), S&P (-0.17%), NAS100 (-0.14%) per 08:38 report. QQQ likely seeing outflows into safer havens. Risk-off mood clearly established.

factors.name: Mega-cap earnings & guidance outlook clouded
factors.summary: Apple commits $30B to Broadcom for US chip manufacturing (positive for capex narrative), but geopolitical uncertainty delays confidence in forward guidance from hyperscalers. Sentiment remains cautious pending FOMC Minutes and CPI confirmation.

factors.name: Semiconductor / AI capex resilience
factors.summary: Apple-Broadcom $30B deal, SambaNova $11B valuation, UBS semiconductor upgrade, and SK Hynix $28B ADR bookbuild oversubscription show strong underlying demand. However, this is being overshadowed by macro headwinds today.

factors.name: FOMC Minutes due today (imminent)
factors.summary: FOMC Minutes release pending (likely late NY morning). Market is choppy ahead of this. MUFG strategists question Fed's hawkish dot plot given weaker labor data and lower energy prices (pre-oil spike). Outcome could flip bias sharply.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD Index near 101.00, supported by oil-driven rate hikes expectations and risk-off flows. Strong Dollar typically headwind for NAS100 (heavy international revenue exposure in MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL). AUD, CNH weakened; DXY firmness sustained.

factors.name: China-linked sentiment
factors.summary: Alibaba surged best in 10 months on earnings rebound hopes; China lifting fuel export restrictions. However, geopolitical risk and rate pressure override near-term optimism. China tensions around Taiwan/AI also persist.

factors.name: Price action context (4h -0.43%, 24h +0.38%)
factors.summary: Small 4h decline but overnight/early morning move was positive (24h +0.38%). Current -0.43% 4h suggests momentum has shifted into selling since NAS100 futures opened lower after Trump Iran comments (~08:37 UTC). Move is in early-to-mid phase, not yet extended.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught in a fresh risk-off cycle triggered by Trump's declared end to the US-Iran ceasefire (~08:27 UTC). Oil surged 3.2–4% on supply fears, reigniting inflation concerns and pressuring long-duration tech valuations via real yield repricing. While semiconductor/AI capex demand signals remain intact (Apple-Broadcom $30B, SambaNova $11B), macro headwinds dominate: stronger Dollar, renewed geopolitical risk premium, and ECB/Fed re-pricing pressures. FOMC Minutes due today could clarify whether Fed sees this as transitory oil shock or material inflation re-acceleration. Bias is SELL into strength, but conviction is moderate given imminent catalyst risk.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Minutes (today, ~14:00 UTC or earlier NY session) — critical for Fed rate-cut/hike repricing
US CPI release (next week) — will determine if oil spike transmits to core inflation
Oil supply developments (Strait of Hormuz, Iran response escalation)
Mega-cap earnings season (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN dates not in immediate window but guidance will hinge on macro clarity)
screening.risk_warning: If FOMC Minutes signal dovish pivot or de-escalation news breaks on Iran, NAS100 could reverse sharply higher. Oil revert sub-$73 on ceasefire restoration would also flip bias BULLISH. Real yields also highly sensitive to any CPI downside surprise before next week's data.
screening.entry_guidance: NAS100 is in PHASE 2 (move in progress, down ~0.43% 4h). Wait for retest of 29300–29400 resistance before shorting; alternatively, enter long only on pullback through 29000–29050 support IF FOMC Minutes are dovish. Avoid chasing the dip into imminent catalyst.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk: US-Iran Ceasefire Collapse
factors.summary: Trump declared Iran ceasefire 'over' after latest strikes (Bloomberg, 10:30 UTC). Oil surged 3.2% above $74 WTI, highest in 2 weeks (FXStreet 09:07); risk-off sentiment triggered broad equity selloff across Asia and emerging markets.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike & Inflation Expectations Reignited
factors.summary: Brent crude jumped from $72 to $76+ on Middle East escalation (BNY, 11:18 UTC). European bonds sold off with French yields hitting 2009 highs, and inflation breakevens rising. Long-duration tech is structurally sensitive to upside real-rate surprises from oil-driven inflation.

factors.name: Real Yields / Duration Risk
factors.summary: Oil spike + inflation fears typically push real yields higher, compressing valuations on high-beta, long-duration Nasdaq 100 constituents (mega-cap unprofitable growth, AI plays). US equities remain 'geared to inflation risk' per BNY iFlow data (09:42 UTC), widening gap between inflation-sensitive flows and falling breakevens.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Multi-Asset Selloff
factors.summary: Gold declined 1.40% to ~$4,050 (09:55 UTC); Indian stocks tumbled, rupee weakened (11:21 UTC); Korean stocks entered bear territory (10:57 UTC); British Pound, Australian Dollar, and NZD all pressured. Risk-off environment typically drags broad Nasdaq lower.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Kevin Warsh Uncertainty
factors.summary: FOMC minutes released today; new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signaled he may reduce policy signaling, introducing 'wild rides for traders' (MarketWatch, 11:00 UTC). No explicit hawkish/dovish surprise yet, but geopolitical shock may shift calculus. Awaiting minutes detail.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Cycle
factors.summary: No major earnings surprises in last 24h window. Goldman noted earnings surprise wave unlikely to repeat (10:50 UTC). Apple announced $30B+ Broadcom capex commitment (10:36 UTC), supporting long-term AI narrative but not immediate catalyst. Awaiting next earnings cycle.

factors.name: AI Rotation & Valuation Pressure
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports AI rotation gathering pace in Asia—investors rotating *out* of chipmakers into cheaper AI plays (11:10 UTC). This suggests sector rebalancing but not Nasdaq collapse; however, if rotation accelerates into growth-heavy constituents, could weigh on index.

factors.name: Price Action: 4h & 24h Context
factors.summary: 4h change: -0.43% (minor decline in progress). 24h change: +0.38% (shallow overnight rally). Move downward is nascent but aligned with fresh geopolitical shock; no extended rally to fade yet.

factors.name: Upcoming Catalysts: FOMC Minutes, CPI Expectations
factors.summary: FOMC minutes already published today (referenced 10:57 UTC), but detailed breakdown may clarify rate outlook. Oil-driven inflation concerns now front-and-center; next CPI/PCE print will be critical to whether Fed pivots dovish or holds hawkish. Warsh's first actions still unknown.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 opened with a modest +0.38% 24h gain but reversed sharply after Trump declared the US-Iran ceasefire 'over' around 10:30 UTC. Oil surged 3.2% to $74+, triggering a broad risk-off selloff across equities, commodities, and EM assets. Real yields are rising on inflation expectations as European bonds sold off heavily; long-duration tech is structurally vulnerable to higher real rates. Prior three daily reads flagged SELL6/10 caution, and today's geopolitical shock appears to be the confirmation catalyst. No major earnings catalyst or dovish Fed surprise to offset the headwind.

Next CPI/Core CPI print (TBD; watch for upside surprise from oil pass-through)

(4) Any major positive earnings surprise from mega-cap tech (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL) overrides near-term macro headwinds.

Consider short entries on retest of 29,250–29,300 resistance or breakdown below 29,050 support, but avoid chasing lower into extended move. Watch FOMC minutes and oil price anchor for confirmation.
